title: Graduate School of Addiction and Forensic Sciences Research and Development
content:
Graduate School of Addiction and Forensic Sciences Research and Development

Studies and Targets within the Scope of R&D (1/2)

Our Graduate School's research strategies and objectives are in line with our University's 2019-2023 Strategic plan.

Our Graduate School's Research Strategy is based on:

-To increase R&D resources and effectiveness,

-Strengthening the culture of science, research, creativity and innovation,

- To support the academic staff and students in the graduate-school with a high motivation to work and research process,

Contributing to technology and universal science by producing new information through research carried out by considering national priorities.

-Producing and disseminating scientific knowledge and making it available to the public,

- To encourage academia and stakeholders to contribute to the country's economy by transforming scientific knowledge into technology,

- To ensure the effective use of Research and Application Centers in postgraduate thesis and project studies and to inform our students about the centers and their duties

- To enable our students to establish international collaborations and academic partnerships, and to encourage academic collaborations.

Studies and Targets in the Scope of R&D (2/2)

In line with the 2019-2023 Strategic Plan of our University

Our PARGE, laboratory, Application and Research Centers provide physical infrastructure and financial resources for research and development activities.

Research activities at our university are monitored through the Activity Tracking System (FTS). Determination, implementation, control and measures of policies and targets for performance improvement are in the planning stage. The 2017-2018 research performance of our faculty is kept as an activity table and is also monitored through the FTS system.

In our university, the Scientific Incentive Directive is also implemented in order to develop the competencies of the faculty members.
